description: FDL Auto-Evolution Hooks — Automatic validation, docs generation, and commits

after-fdl-extract-code.description: After extracting blueprints from code, auto-validate and commit

after-fdl-create.description: After creating a new blueprint, auto-validate and commit

before-git-push.description: Before pushing, ensure all blueprints are valid and docs are current
before-git-push.note: Enable this to enforce validation on every push

watch-blueprints.description: Watch blueprints directory and auto-evolve on changes
watch-blueprints.trigger: whenever any *.blueprint.yaml file changes in blueprints/
watch-blueprints.note: Enable this for real-time evolution as you edit blueprints

after-fdl-propagate-agi.description: After AGI propagation, auto-validate and commit

after-schema-change.description: When AGI schema changes, remind to propagate to all blueprints

research-reminder.trigger: whenever auto-evolve commits more than 10 blueprints in a session
research-reminder.action: suggest /fdl-research to check for latest AI patterns

post-edit-linters.description: Run framework-appropriate linter/formatter on FDL-generated source files (files containing `// FDL:` audit comment). Silent on failure so missing linters don't block generation.
post-edit-linters.trigger: whenever /fdl-generate writes or edits a file containing an `// FDL:` audit comment
post-edit-linters.action: dispatch by extension — .ts/.tsx → 'npx eslint --fix {file} 2>/dev/null || true'; .py → 'ruff check --fix {file} 2>/dev/null || true'; .go → 'gofmt -w {file}'; .rs → 'rustfmt {file} 2>/dev/null || true'; .cs → 'dotnet format --include {file} 2>/dev/null || true'; .php → 'php-cs-fixer fix {file} 2>/dev/null || true'
post-edit-linters.note: Experimental. Enable per-project once you have the linters installed. The FDL: marker scope prevents this from touching pre-existing code. To wire into Claude Code natively, mirror this into .claude/settings.json under hooks.PostToolUse with a matcher that checks the written file for the FDL: comment.

validate-on-change.description: Automatically run validation after any blueprint modification

generate-docs-on-change.description: Automatically regenerate docs when blueprints change

commit-on-valid-blueprints.description: Create atomic commits when all blueprints are valid

what-this-means.Without hooks: Manual workflow: edit blueprint → run validate → run generate → git add → git commit
what-this-means.With hooks: Automatic workflow: edit blueprint → (validation, docs, commit happen automatically)
what-this-means.Result: FDL becomes an evolving system where improvements automatically propagate through the entire pipeline
